korean: 저는 오늘 회의가 있었습니다.
english: I had a meeting today.
chunk: I had a meeting / today.
substitution_drills: I had a call today.
I had a deadline today.

korean: 저는 오늘 데이터를 확인했습니다.
english: I checked the data today.
chunk: I checked the data / today.
substitution_drills: I sent the report today.
I ran the assay today.

korean: 저는 오늘 팀과 결과를 공유했습니다.
english: I shared the results with my team today.
chunk: I shared the results / with my team / today.
substitution_drills: I discussed the plan with my boss today.
I reviewed the draft with my team today.

korean: 저는 오늘 아침에 데이터를 확인하려고 회의를 했습니다.
english: I had a meeting this morning to check the data.
chunk: I had a meeting / this morning / to check the data.
substitution_drills: I had a call this morning to confirm the scope.
I had a review this afternoon to finalize the plan.

korean: 저는 오늘 결과를 공유하려고 팀과 회의를 했는데, 생각보다 길어졌습니다.
english: I had a meeting with my team to share the results, but it ran long.
chunk: I had a meeting with my team / to share the results / but it ran long.
substitution_drills: I had a call with the vendor to fix the issue, but it ran long.
I had a review with my boss to confirm the plan, but it got delayed.

korean: 저는 그것을 끝내려고 했습니다.
english: I tried to finish it.
chunk: I tried to finish / it.
substitution_drills: I tried to fix it.
I tried to explain it.

korean: 저는 그 문제를 해결하려고 했습니다.
english: I tried to solve the problem.
chunk: I tried to solve / the problem.
substitution_drills: I tried to repeat the assay.
I tried to reach the vendor.

korean: 저는 마감 전에 그것을 끝내려고 했습니다.
english: I tried to finish it before the deadline.
chunk: I tried to finish it / before the deadline.
substitution_drills: I tried to send it before the meeting.
I tried to confirm it before today.

korean: 저는 그 문제를 직접 해결하려고 했지만, 잘 안 됐습니다.
english: I tried to solve the problem myself, but it didn't work.
chunk: I tried to solve the problem myself / but it didn't work.
pattern: I tried to [verb] [object] myself, but it didn't work.
substitution_drills: I tried to fix the assay myself, but it didn't work.
I tried to explain it clearly, but it didn't work.

korean: 저는 마감 전에 그것을 끝내려고 했지만, 데이터가 늦게 와서 못 했습니다.
english: I tried to finish it before the deadline, but the data came late.
chunk: I tried to finish it before the deadline / but the data came late.
substitution_drills: I tried to send it before the meeting, but the file was too large.
I tried to confirm it before today, but the vendor was slow.

korean: 그것은 어려웠습니다.
english: It was hard.
chunk: It was / hard.
substitution_drills: It was busy.
It was fine.

korean: 그 회의는 길었습니다.
english: The meeting was long.
chunk: The meeting was / long.
substitution_drills: The day was busy.
The result was clear.

korean: 그 회의는 길었지만 유용했습니다.
english: The meeting was long but useful.
chunk: The meeting was long / but useful.
substitution_drills: The day was busy but productive.
The talk was short but clear.

korean: 데이터가 많아서 그것은 저에게 어려웠습니다.
english: It was hard for me because there was a lot of data.
chunk: It was hard for me / because there was a lot of data.
substitution_drills: It was busy for me because there were many calls.
It was easy for me because the scope was small.

korean: 그 회의는 길었지만, 다음 단계를 정해서 유용했습니다.
english: The meeting was long, but it was useful because we set the next steps.
chunk: The meeting was long / but it was useful / because we set the next steps.
pattern: The [thing] was [adjective], but it was useful because [reason].
substitution_drills: The day was busy, but it was good because we finished the report.
The review was hard, but it was helpful because we found the issue.

korean: 저는 쉬고 싶습니다.
english: I want to rest.
chunk: I want to / rest.
substitution_drills: I want to start.
I want to learn.

korean: 저는 영어를 늘리고 싶습니다.
english: I want to improve my English.
chunk: I want to improve / my English.
substitution_drills: I want to finish the project.
I want to lead the study.

korean: 저는 회의 전에 데이터를 확인하고 싶습니다.
english: I want to check the data before the meeting.
chunk: I want to check the data / before the meeting.
substitution_drills: I want to review the plan before the call.
I want to confirm the scope before kickoff.

korean: 저는 결정하기 전에 더 많은 데이터를 보고 싶습니다.
english: I want to see more data before we decide.
chunk: I want to see more data / before we decide.
substitution_drills: I want to run one more test before we conclude.
I want to align internally before we respond.

korean: 저는 회의에서 더 자신 있게 말하기 위해 영어를 늘리고 싶습니다.
english: I want to improve my English so I can speak with more confidence in meetings.
chunk: I want to improve my English / so I can speak with more confidence / in meetings.
pattern: I want to [verb] so I can [goal].
substitution_drills: I want to practice more so I can present without notes.
I want to prepare early so I can answer questions calmly.

korean: 저는 가야 합니다.
english: I need to go.
chunk: I need to / go.
substitution_drills: I need to check.
I need to decide.

korean: 저는 이 보고서를 끝내야 합니다.
english: I need to finish this report.
chunk: I need to finish / this report.
substitution_drills: I need to send this email.
I need to repeat this assay.

korean: 저는 답하기 전에 이것을 확인해야 합니다.
english: I need to check this before I reply.
chunk: I need to check this / before I reply.
pattern: I need to [verb] this before I [verb].
substitution_drills: I need to confirm this before I commit.
I need to review this before I send it.

korean: 저는 진행하기 전에 팀과 이것을 맞춰야 합니다.
english: I need to align with my team before we move forward.
chunk: I need to align with my team / before we move forward.
pattern: I need to [verb] with [person] before we [verb].
substitution_drills: I need to check with the vendor before we confirm.
I need to talk with my boss before we decide.

korean: 저는 결정하기 전에 이 데이터를 확인해야 하는데, 재현이 되는지 봐야 합니다.
english: I need to check this data before we decide, because I want to see if it is reproducible.
chunk: I need to check this data before we decide / because I want to see if it is reproducible.
pattern: I need to [verb] before we [verb], because [reason].
substitution_drills: I need to confirm the scope before we commit, because the budget is tight.
I need to align the team before we respond, because the timeline is short.

korean: 저는 그것이 좋다고 생각합니다.
english: I think it is good.
chunk: I think / it is good.
substitution_drills: I think it is risky.
I think it is enough.

korean: 저는 우리가 기다려야 한다고 생각합니다.
english: I think we should wait.
chunk: I think / we should wait.
substitution_drills: I think we should repeat it.
I think we should align first.

korean: 저는 이 옵션이 더 안전하다고 생각합니다.
english: I think this option is safer.
chunk: I think / this option is safer.
substitution_drills: I think this plan is simpler.
I think this approach is clearer.

korean: 저는 결정하기 전에 더 기다려야 한다고 생각합니다.
english: I think we should wait before we decide.
chunk: I think we should wait / before we decide.
pattern: I think we should [verb] before we [verb].
substitution_drills: I think we should test before we conclude.
I think we should align before we respond.

korean: 저는 이 옵션이 더 안전하다고 생각하는데, 리스크를 더 잘 통제할 수 있기 때문입니다.
english: I think this option is safer, because we can control the risk better.
chunk: I think this option is safer / because we can control the risk better.
substitution_drills: I think this plan is simpler, because it needs fewer steps.
I think this approach is clearer, because it shows the trend better.

korean: 저는 확실하지 않습니다.
english: I'm not sure.
chunk: I'm not sure.
substitution_drills: I'm not certain.
I'm not convinced.

korean: 저는 그것이 맞는지 확실하지 않습니다.
english: I'm not sure if it is correct.
chunk: I'm not sure / if it is correct.
substitution_drills: I'm not sure if it is enough.
I'm not sure if it is ready.

korean: 저는 이것이 최선의 방법인지 확실하지 않습니다.
english: I'm not sure if this is the best way.
chunk: I'm not sure / if this is the best way.
pattern: I'm not sure if this is the [superlative] [noun].
substitution_drills: I'm not sure if this is the right target.
I'm not sure if this is the safest option.

korean: 저는 그 단어가 정확히 기억나지 않지만, 설명해 보겠습니다.
english: I'm not sure of the exact word, but let me explain.
chunk: I'm not sure of the exact word / but let me explain.
pattern: I'm not sure of [thing], but let me [verb].
substitution_drills: I'm not sure of the exact number, but let me estimate.
I'm not sure of the exact date, but let me check.

korean: 저는 이 데이터가 충분한지 확실하지 않아서, 한 번 더 확인하고 싶습니다.
english: I'm not sure if this data is enough, so I want to check it one more time.
chunk: I'm not sure if this data is enough / so I want to check it one more time.
pattern: I'm not sure if [clause], so I want to [verb].
substitution_drills: I'm not sure if the trend is real, so I want to repeat the assay.
I'm not sure if the scope is clear, so I want to confirm it first.

korean: 맞는 말씀이지만, 한 가지 걱정이 있습니다.
english: You're right, but I have one concern.
chunk: You're right / but I have one concern.
substitution_drills: You're right, but I have one question.
You're right, but I see one risk.

korean: 그 점은 이해하지만, 시간이 걱정됩니다.
english: I see your point, but I worry about the time.
chunk: I see your point / but I worry about the time.
pattern: I see your point, but I worry about [thing].
substitution_drills: I see your point, but I worry about the cost.
I see your point, but I worry about the scope.

korean: 그 말씀에 동의하지만, 데이터가 더 필요합니다.
english: I agree with you, but we need more data.
chunk: I agree with you / but we need more data.
pattern: I agree with you, but we need [thing].
substitution_drills: I agree with you, but we need more time.
I agree with you, but we need a control.

korean: 그 접근이 더 빠른 건 맞지만, 해석 리스크가 있습니다.
english: You're right that it is faster, but it has interpretation risk.
chunk: You're right that it is faster / but it has interpretation risk.
substitution_drills: You're right that it is cheaper, but it has quality risk.
You're right that it is simpler, but it has coverage risk.

korean: 그 점은 이해하지만, 결정하기 전에 데이터를 한 번 더 보는 게 더 안전하다고 생각합니다.
english: I see your point, but I think it is safer to check the data once more before we decide.
chunk: I see your point / but I think it is safer to check the data once more / before we decide.
pattern: I see your point, but I think it is safer to [verb] before we [verb].
substitution_drills: I see your point, but I think it is safer to test once more before we commit.
I see your point, but I think it is safer to align first before we respond.

korean: 도와주실 수 있나요?
english: Can you help me?
chunk: Can you / help me?
substitution_drills: Can you call me?
Can you join me?

korean: 그 파일을 보내주실 수 있나요?
english: Can you send the file?
chunk: Can you send / the file?
substitution_drills: Can you share the data?
Can you check the result?

korean: 내일까지 그 파일을 보내주실 수 있나요?
english: Can you send the file by tomorrow?
chunk: Can you send the file / by tomorrow?
substitution_drills: Can you share the data by Friday?
Can you confirm the scope by today?

korean: 회의 전에 그 데이터를 다시 확인해 주실 수 있나요?
english: Can you check the data again before the meeting?
chunk: Can you check the data again / before the meeting?
substitution_drills: Can you review the draft again before the call?
Can you run the test again before kickoff?

korean: 회신하기 전에 이 부분을 다시 확인하고 무엇이 빠졌는지 알려주실 수 있나요?
english: Can you check this part again and let me know what is missing before we reply?
chunk: Can you check this part again / and let me know what is missing / before we reply?
pattern: Can you [verb] and let me know [clause] before we [verb]?
substitution_drills: Can you review the scope and tell me what is unclear before we commit?
Can you read the draft and mark what is weak before we send it?

korean: 문제는 시간입니다.
english: The problem is time.
chunk: The problem is / time.
substitution_drills: The problem is cost.
The problem is scope.

korean: 문제는 데이터가 부족하다는 것입니다.
english: The problem is that we don't have enough data.
chunk: The problem is / that we don't have enough data.
substitution_drills: The problem is that we don't have a control.
The problem is that we don't have time.

korean: 문제는 일정이 너무 촉박하다는 것입니다.
english: The problem is that the timeline is too tight.
chunk: The problem is / that the timeline is too tight.
pattern: The problem is that the [thing] is too [adjective].
substitution_drills: The problem is that the budget is too small.
The problem is that the window is too narrow.

korean: 문제는 우리가 아직 타깃을 정하지 않았다는 것입니다.
english: The problem is that we have not decided the target yet.
chunk: The problem is / that we have not decided the target yet.
pattern: The problem is that we have not [past participle] yet.
substitution_drills: The problem is that we have not confirmed the scope yet.
The problem is that we have not received the data yet.

korean: 문제는 일정이 촉박하다는 것인데, 그래서 우선순위를 먼저 정해야 합니다.
english: The problem is that the timeline is tight, so we need to set the priority first.
chunk: The problem is that the timeline is tight / so we need to set the priority first.
pattern: The problem is that [clause], so we need to [verb] first.
substitution_drills: The problem is that the budget is small, so we need to cut the scope first.
The problem is that the data is limited, so we need to repeat the assay first.

korean: 데이터는 차이를 보여줍니다.
english: The data show a difference.
chunk: The data show / a difference.
substitution_drills: The data show a trend.
The data show an effect.

korean: 데이터는 명확한 경향을 보여줍니다.
english: The data show a clear trend.
chunk: The data show / a clear trend.
substitution_drills: The data show a small effect.
The data show a strong signal.

korean: 데이터는 클론 간의 명확한 차이를 보여줍니다.
english: The data show a clear difference between the clones.
chunk: The data show a clear difference / between the clones.
pattern: The data show a [adjective] difference between [items].
substitution_drills: The data show a clear gap between the formats.
The data show a clear shift between the conditions.

korean: 데이터는 경향을 보여주지만, 효과 크기는 작습니다.
english: The data show a trend, but the effect size is small.
chunk: The data show a trend / but the effect size is small.
substitution_drills: The data show a signal, but the difference is small.
The data show an effect, but the variability is high.

korean: 데이터는 클론 간 명확한 차이를 보여주지만, 한 번의 실험이라 아직 조심스럽습니다.
english: The data show a clear difference between the clones, but I am still cautious because it is only one run.
chunk: The data show a clear difference between the clones / but I am still cautious / because it is only one run.
pattern: The data show [finding], but I am still cautious because [reason].
substitution_drills: The data show a strong signal, but I am still cautious because the control is weak.
The data show a clear trend, but I am still cautious because the sample size is small.

korean: A가 B보다 높습니다.
english: A is higher than B.
chunk: A is higher / than B.
substitution_drills: Clone 1 is higher than clone 2.
The signal is higher than the background.

korean: 이 클론은 다른 클론보다 약합니다.
english: This clone is weaker than the others.
chunk: This clone is weaker / than the others.
substitution_drills: This format is more stable than the others.
This condition is cleaner than the others.

korean: 첫 번째 클론의 신호가 두 번째보다 훨씬 높습니다.
english: The signal of the first clone is much higher than the second.
chunk: The signal of the first clone is much higher / than the second.
substitution_drills: The affinity of the lead clone is much higher than the backup.
The background of this plate is much higher than the control.

korean: A는 B보다 높지만, 그 차이는 크지 않습니다.
english: A is higher than B, but the difference is not large.
chunk: A is higher than B / but the difference is not large.
pattern: [A] is higher than [B], but the difference is not [adjective].
substitution_drills: Clone 1 is higher than clone 2, but the difference is not clear.
The signal is higher than the control, but the difference is not strong.

korean: 절대값은 클론 1이 더 높지만, 순위는 두 assay에서 동일하게 유지됩니다.
english: The absolute value is higher for clone 1, but the ranking stays the same in both assays.
chunk: The absolute value is higher for clone 1 / but the ranking stays the same / in both assays.
pattern: The [metric] is higher for [item], but the ranking stays the same in [context].
substitution_drills: The signal is higher for the lead, but the order stays the same across runs.
The MFI is higher for this clone, but the ranking stays the same in both formats.

korean: 이것은 유용하지만 충분하지 않습니다.
english: This is useful but not enough.
chunk: This is useful / but not enough.
substitution_drills: This is helpful but not final.
This is clear but not complete.

korean: 이 결과는 유용하지만 결정적이지는 않습니다.
english: This result is useful but not conclusive.
chunk: This result is useful / but not conclusive.
substitution_drills: This assay is useful but not decisive.
This trend is useful but not definitive.

korean: 이 데이터는 방향을 보여주지만 확증은 아닙니다.
english: This data suggests a direction but does not confirm it.
chunk: This data suggests a direction / but does not confirm it.
pattern: This data suggests [noun] but does not confirm it.
substitution_drills: This data suggests binding but does not confirm function.
This data suggests activity but does not confirm mechanism.

korean: 이 결과는 후보 선별에는 유용하지만, 단독 근거로는 부족합니다.
english: This result is useful for triage but not sufficient as standalone evidence.
chunk: This result is useful for triage / but not sufficient as standalone evidence.
pattern: This result is useful for [purpose] but not sufficient as [role].
substitution_drills: This result is useful for screening but not sufficient as proof.
This result is useful for ranking but not sufficient as confirmation.

korean: 이 결과는 후보를 좁히는 데는 유용하지만, 직교 assay로 확인하기 전에는 단독 근거로 쓰지 않겠습니다.
english: This result is useful for narrowing candidates, but I would not use it as standalone evidence before we confirm it with an orthogonal assay.
chunk: This result is useful for narrowing candidates / but I would not use it as standalone evidence / before we confirm it with an orthogonal assay.
pattern: This result is useful for [purpose], but I would not use it as [role] before [condition].
substitution_drills: This result is useful for early ranking, but I would not use it as proof before we repeat the run.
This result is useful for triage, but I would not use it as confirmation before we add a control.

korean: 아직 확정할 수 없습니다.
english: We can't confirm it yet.
chunk: We can't confirm it / yet.
substitution_drills: We can't rank them yet.
We can't decide yet.

korean: 우리는 아직 메커니즘을 확정할 수 없습니다.
english: We can't confirm the mechanism yet.
chunk: We can't confirm / the mechanism yet.
substitution_drills: We can't confirm the ranking yet.
We can't confirm the effect yet.

korean: 이 데이터만으로는 메커니즘을 확정할 수 없습니다.
english: We can't confirm the mechanism from this data alone.
chunk: We can't confirm the mechanism / from this data alone.
substitution_drills: We can't confirm activity from binding alone.
We can't confirm the ranking from one assay alone.

korean: 재현 데이터를 보기 전에는 순위를 확정할 수 없습니다.
english: We can't confirm the ranking before we see the repeat data.
chunk: We can't confirm the ranking / before we see the repeat data.
pattern: We can't confirm [noun] before we see [evidence].
substitution_drills: We can't confirm the effect before we see the control.
We can't confirm the trend before we see the second run.

korean: 같은 경향이 다른 assay에서도 재현되는지 보기 전에는 이것을 메커니즘으로 확정할 수 없습니다.
english: We can't confirm this as the mechanism before we see whether the same trend appears in another assay.
chunk: We can't confirm this as the mechanism / before we see whether the same trend appears / in another assay.
pattern: We can't confirm [claim] before we see whether [condition].
substitution_drills: We can't confirm this as the effect before we see whether it holds in a second run.
We can't confirm this as specific before we see whether the control stays clean.

korean: 경향은 명확합니다.
english: The trend is clear.
chunk: The trend is / clear.
substitution_drills: The trend is weak.
The trend is consistent.

korean: 경향은 보이지만 약합니다.
english: The trend is visible but weak.
chunk: The trend is visible / but weak.
substitution_drills: The trend is clear but small.
The trend is real but noisy.

korean: 경향은 두 실험에서 일관되게 나타납니다.
english: The trend is consistent across the two runs.
chunk: The trend is consistent / across the two runs.
substitution_drills: The trend is consistent across the formats.
The trend is consistent across the conditions.

korean: 경향은 일관되지만, 효과 크기는 여전히 작습니다.
english: The trend is consistent, but the effect size is still small.
chunk: The trend is consistent / but the effect size is still small.
pattern: The trend is [adjective], but [qualifier] is still [adjective].
substitution_drills: The trend is clear, but the difference is still minor.
The trend is stable, but the signal is still low.

korean: 경향은 두 실험에서 일관되게 나타나지만, 결론을 내리기 전에 직교 assay로 한 번 더 확인하고 싶습니다.
english: The trend is consistent across the two runs, but I want to confirm it with an orthogonal assay before we conclude.
chunk: The trend is consistent across the two runs / but I want to confirm it with an orthogonal assay / before we conclude.
pattern: The trend is consistent across [context], but I want to confirm it with [method] before we [verb].
substitution_drills: The trend is consistent across the formats, but I want to confirm it with a functional assay before we nominate.
The trend is consistent across the runs, but I want to confirm it with a larger panel before we decide.

korean: 우리는 먼저 확인해야 합니다.
english: We should check first.
chunk: We should check / first.
substitution_drills: We should align first.
We should repeat first.

korean: 우리는 먼저 재현성을 확인해야 합니다.
english: We should confirm reproducibility first.
chunk: We should confirm reproducibility / first.
substitution_drills: We should check specificity first.
We should review the controls first.

korean: 후보를 정하기 전에 우리는 먼저 경향을 확인해야 합니다.
english: We should confirm the trend first before we select candidates.
chunk: We should confirm the trend first / before we select candidates.
substitution_drills: We should check binding first before we reformat.
We should align scope first before we contact the vendor.

korean: 우리는 결론을 보류하고 먼저 확인 실험을 진행해야 합니다.
english: We should hold the conclusion and run confirmation first.
chunk: We should hold the conclusion / and run confirmation first.
substitution_drills: We should hold the ranking and repeat the assay first.
We should hold the nomination and check the controls first.

korean: 후보를 정하기 전에 같은 경향이 재현되는지 먼저 확인하고, 그다음에 reformatting을 진행하는 게 좋겠습니다.
english: We should confirm that the same trend is reproducible first, and then move to reformatting before we select candidates.
chunk: We should confirm that the same trend is reproducible first / and then move to reformatting / before we select candidates.
pattern: We should [verb] first, and then [verb] before we [verb].
substitution_drills: We should check specificity first, and then run the functional assay before we nominate.
We should confirm binding first, and then start developability before we rank.

korean: 시작하기 전에 계획이 필요합니다.
english: Before we start, we need a plan.
chunk: Before we start / we need a plan.
substitution_drills: Before we start, we need a control.
Before we start, we need approval.

korean: 결정하기 전에 더 많은 데이터가 필요합니다.
english: Before we decide, we need more data.
chunk: Before we decide / we need more data.
substitution_drills: Before we conclude, we need more runs.
Before we commit, we need more time.

korean: reformatting 전에 명확한 선정 기준이 필요합니다.
english: Before reformatting, we need clear selection criteria.
chunk: Before reformatting / we need clear selection criteria.
substitution_drills: Before screening, we need clear cut-offs.
Before nomination, we need clear ranking rules.

korean: 후보를 좁히기 전에 우리는 control 데이터를 먼저 확인해야 합니다.
english: Before we narrow the candidates, we need to check the control data.
chunk: Before we narrow the candidates / we need to check the control data.
substitution_drills: Before we rank the clones, we need to check the assay window.
Before we nominate the lead, we need to check the specificity.

korean: 96개를 reformatting하기 전에, 어떤 기준으로 후보를 고를지 먼저 합의해야 시간과 비용을 아낄 수 있습니다.
english: Before we reformat the 96 candidates, we need to agree on the selection criteria first, so we can save time and cost.
chunk: Before we reformat the 96 candidates / we need to agree on the selection criteria first / so we can save time and cost.
pattern: Before we [activity], we need to agree on [noun] first, so we can [benefit].
substitution_drills: Before we start the panel, we need to agree on the cut-offs first, so we can avoid rework.
Before we order the materials, we need to agree on the scope first, so we can control the budget.

korean: 이것은 도움이 될 수 있습니다.
english: This may help.
chunk: This may / help.
substitution_drills: This may work.
This may change.

korean: 긴 incubation은 background를 높일 수 있습니다.
english: Longer incubation may increase the background.
chunk: Longer incubation may increase / the background.
substitution_drills: A narrow range may weaken the fit.
Low affinity may reduce the signal.

korean: 이 차이는 assay artifact일 수도 있습니다.
english: This difference may be an assay artifact.
chunk: This difference may be / an assay artifact.
substitution_drills: This signal may be background.
This shift may be a fitting error.

korean: 낮은 CD3 affinity는 safety를 개선할 수 있지만, potency를 낮출 수도 있습니다.
english: Lower CD3 affinity may improve safety, but it may also reduce potency.
chunk: Lower CD3 affinity may improve safety / but it may also reduce potency.
substitution_drills: A higher dose may increase potency, but it may also raise toxicity.
Longer incubation may raise the signal, but it may also raise the background.

korean: 이 차이는 실제 클론 차이일 수도 있지만 assay artifact일 수도 있어서, raw data를 확인한 뒤에 판단하겠습니다.
english: This difference may be a real clone difference, but it may also be an assay artifact, so I will judge after I check the raw data.
chunk: This difference may be a real clone difference / but it may also be an assay artifact / so I will judge after I check the raw data.
pattern: This may be [A], but it may also be [B], so I will [verb] after [condition].
substitution_drills: This signal may be specific, but it may also be background, so I will decide after I check the control.
This shift may be real, but it may also be noise, so I will conclude after I repeat the run.

korean: 시작합시다.
english: Let's start.
chunk: Let's / start.
substitution_drills: Let's wait.
Let's proceed.

korean: 가장 단순한 조건부터 시작합시다.
english: Let's start with the simplest condition.
chunk: Let's start / with the simplest condition.
substitution_drills: Let's start with the easiest target.
Let's start with the smallest panel.

korean: 필수 항목과 선택 항목을 나눠 봅시다.
english: Let's separate the required items from the optional ones.
chunk: Let's separate the required items / from the optional ones.
substitution_drills: Let's separate the core assays from the optional ones.
Let's separate the confirmed data from the assumptions.

korean: 진행하기 전에 단순한 co-culture 조건부터 맞춰 봅시다.
english: Let's align on a simple co-culture condition before we proceed.
chunk: Let's align on a simple co-culture condition / before we proceed.
substitution_drills: Let's align on a basic readout before we scale up.
Let's align on a single dose before we expand.

korean: 한 번에 모든 조건을 바꾸지 말고, 가장 단순한 조건부터 시작해서 하나씩 추가해 봅시다.
english: Let's not change every condition at once, but start with the simplest one and add variables step by step.
chunk: Let's not change every condition at once / but start with the simplest one / and add variables step by step.
pattern: Let's not [verb] at once, but start with [noun] and [verb] step by step.
substitution_drills: Let's not test every dose at once, but start with the lowest one and raise it step by step.
Let's not screen the full panel at once, but start with a subset and expand step by step.

korean: 저는 내일까지 보내겠습니다.
english: I will send it by tomorrow.
chunk: I will send it / by tomorrow.
substitution_drills: I will finish it by Friday.
I will confirm it by today.

korean: 저는 이번 주 안에 업데이트 버전을 공유하겠습니다.
english: I will share the updated version within this week.
chunk: I will share the updated version / within this week.
substitution_drills: I will send the summary within today.
I will deliver the draft within two days.

korean: 저는 회의가 끝난 후에 요약본을 보내겠습니다.
english: I will send the summary after the meeting.
chunk: I will send the summary / after the meeting.
substitution_drills: I will share the notes after the call.
I will update the table after the review.

korean: 저는 재현 데이터를 확인한 후에 최종 의견을 공유하겠습니다.
english: I will share my final view after I check the repeat data.
chunk: I will share my final view / after I check the repeat data.
substitution_drills: I will share my recommendation after I review the controls.
I will share the ranking after I confirm the trend.

korean: 저는 재현 데이터를 확인한 뒤에 최종 의견을 드리겠지만, 그 전에라도 주요 리스크는 먼저 정리해서 공유하겠습니다.
english: I will share my final view after I check the repeat data, but I will summarize the key risks and send them first even before that.
chunk: I will share my final view after I check the repeat data / but I will summarize the key risks / and send them first even before that.
pattern: I will [verb] after [condition], but I will [verb] first even before that.
substitution_drills: I will give my recommendation after the second run, but I will flag the main concern first even before that.
I will confirm the ranking after the panel, but I will share the top candidates first even before that.

korean: 확인 부탁드립니다.
english: Please confirm.
chunk: Please / confirm.
substitution_drills: Please clarify.
Please advise.

korean: assay 셋업을 확인해 주세요.
english: Please confirm the assay setup.
chunk: Please confirm / the assay setup.
substitution_drills: Please share the timeline.
Please review the scope.

korean: 진행하기 전에 assay 셋업을 확인해 주세요.
english: Please confirm the assay setup before we proceed.
chunk: Please confirm the assay setup / before we proceed.
substitution_drills: Please share the quote before we commit.
Please review the draft before we sign.

korean: 각 activity의 deliverable을 더 구체적으로 명시해 주세요.
english: Please specify the deliverables for each activity in more detail.
chunk: Please specify the deliverables / for each activity / in more detail.
pattern: Please specify [object] for [scope] in more detail.
substitution_drills: Please specify the timeline for each milestone in more detail.
Please specify the cost for each step in more detail.

korean: 견적을 검토하기 전에, 각 activity의 deliverable과 일정을 더 구체적으로 명시해 주시면 내부 검토가 수월할 것 같습니다.
english: Please specify the deliverables and timeline for each activity in more detail, so our internal review can go smoothly before we evaluate the quote.
chunk: Please specify the deliverables and timeline for each activity in more detail / so our internal review can go smoothly / before we evaluate the quote.
pattern: Please specify [object] in more detail, so [benefit] before we [verb].
substitution_drills: Please specify the scope and assumptions in more detail, so our team can align before we respond.
Please specify the milestones and payment terms in more detail, so our review can proceed before we sign.

korean: 명확히 해 주실 수 있나요?
english: Could you clarify?
chunk: Could you / clarify?
substitution_drills: Could you confirm?
Could you explain?

korean: 이 부분을 명확히 해 주실 수 있나요?
english: Could you clarify this part?
chunk: Could you clarify / this part?
substitution_drills: Could you clarify the scope?
Could you clarify the cost?

korean: NGS 요약에 무엇이 포함되는지 명확히 해 주실 수 있나요?
english: Could you clarify what is included in the NGS summary?
chunk: Could you clarify / what is included in the NGS summary?
pattern: Could you clarify what is included in [object]?
substitution_drills: Could you clarify what is included in the final report?
Could you clarify what is included in the base price?

korean: NGS로 진행하면 Sanger가 제외되는 구조인지 명확히 해 주실 수 있나요?
english: Could you clarify whether Sanger is excluded if we go with NGS?
chunk: Could you clarify / whether Sanger is excluded / if we go with NGS?
substitution_drills: Could you clarify whether shipping is included if we order in bulk?
Could you clarify whether revisions are covered if we extend the scope?

korean: 최종 보고서에 raw data가 포함되는지, 그리고 NGS로 가면 Sanger가 빠지는 구조인지 명확히 해 주시면 내부에서 결정하기 쉬울 것 같습니다.
english: Could you clarify whether raw data are included in the final report, and whether Sanger is dropped if we go with NGS, so it is easier for us to decide internally?
chunk: Could you clarify whether raw data are included in the final report / and whether Sanger is dropped if we go with NGS / so it is easier for us to decide internally?
pattern: Could you clarify whether [A] and whether [B], so [benefit]?
substitution_drills: Could you clarify whether training is included and whether support continues after delivery, so we can plan ahead?
Could you clarify whether controls are included and whether repeats are covered, so we can finalize the scope?

korean: 예산 때문에 우리는 조정해야 합니다.
english: Because of the budget, we need to adjust.
chunk: Because of the budget / we need to adjust.
substitution_drills: Because of the timeline, we need to prioritize.
Because of the risk, we need to wait.

korean: 예산이 제한적이라 scope를 줄여야 합니다.
english: Because the budget is limited, we need to reduce the scope.
chunk: Because the budget is limited / we need to reduce the scope.
substitution_drills: Because the timeline is short, we need to cut the panel.
Because the data is limited, we need to repeat the run.

korean: 예산이 제한적이라, 필수 업무와 선택 업무를 나눠서 견적을 받고 싶습니다.
english: Because the budget is limited, we want to separate the required work from the optional work in the quote.
chunk: Because the budget is limited / we want to separate the required work from the optional work / in the quote.
pattern: Because [clause], we want to separate [items] from [items].
substitution_drills: Because the scope is large, we want to separate phase one from phase two in the plan.
Because the timeline is tight, we want to separate the core assays from the add-ons in the schedule.

korean: 우리 예산이 제한적이라, 이 범위가 현실적인 타협안이라고 생각합니다.
english: Because our budget is limited, I think this scope is a realistic compromise.
chunk: Because our budget is limited / I think this scope is a realistic compromise.
pattern: Because [clause], I think [option] is a realistic compromise.
substitution_drills: Because our timeline is fixed, I think this plan is a reasonable compromise.
Because our resources are limited, I think this panel is a practical compromise.

korean: 우리 예산이 제한적이라 scope 조정이 필요한데, 어떤 단계를 줄이면 비용이 가장 많이 절감되는지 제안해 주실 수 있나요?
english: Because our budget is limited, we need to adjust the scope, so could you suggest which steps would save the most cost if we remove them?
chunk: Because our budget is limited / we need to adjust the scope / so could you suggest which steps would save the most cost?
pattern: Because [clause], we need to [verb], so could you suggest [question]?
substitution_drills: Because our timeline is short, we need to cut some work, so could you suggest which assays we can defer?
Because our budget is fixed, we need to reduce the panel, so could you suggest which targets we can drop?

korean: 대신 기다려도 될까요?
english: Can we wait instead?
chunk: Can we wait / instead?
substitution_drills: Can we proceed instead?
Can we simplify instead?

korean: 대신 더 단순한 옵션을 쓸 수 있을까요?
english: Can we use a simpler option instead?
chunk: Can we use a simpler option / instead?
substitution_drills: Can we use a smaller panel instead?
Can we use a cheaper format instead?

korean: 전체 패널 대신 작은 부분집합으로 시작할 수 있을까요?
english: Can we start with a small subset instead of the full panel?
chunk: Can we start with a small subset / instead of the full panel?
pattern: Can we [verb] with [option A] instead of [option B]?
substitution_drills: Can we go with NGS instead of Sanger?
Can we run a pilot instead of the full study?

korean: 이 옵션은 열어두되, 필수 조건으로 보지 않으면 어떨까요?
english: Can we keep this option open instead of treating it as required?
chunk: Can we keep this option open / instead of treating it as required?
pattern: Can we [verb] instead of [verb-ing] it as [role]?
substitution_drills: Can we list cyno testing as optional instead of including it as standard?
Can we mark this assay as a backup instead of setting it as the primary?

korean: 예산을 맞추기 위해, 전체 패널을 한 번에 진행하는 대신 우선순위가 높은 후보부터 단계적으로 진행할 수 있을까요?
english: To fit the budget, can we run the high-priority candidates in phases instead of processing the full panel at once?
chunk: To fit the budget / can we run the high-priority candidates in phases / instead of processing the full panel at once?
pattern: To [goal], can we [verb] in phases instead of [verb-ing] at once?
substitution_drills: To save cost, can we test the top targets in stages instead of screening everything at once?
To reduce risk, can we confirm the lead first in steps instead of advancing all candidates at once?

korean: 받으면 진행하겠습니다.
english: Once we receive it, we will proceed.
chunk: Once we receive it / we will proceed.
substitution_drills: Once we confirm, we will start.
Once we align, we will respond.

korean: 샘플을 받으면 확인 메일을 드리겠습니다.
english: Once we receive the samples, we will confirm by email.
chunk: Once we receive the samples / we will confirm by email.
substitution_drills: Once we receive the quote, we will reply with comments.
Once we receive the data, we will share our view.

korean: 서류를 받으면 내부적으로 절차를 진행하겠습니다.
english: Once we receive the documents, we will proceed internally.
chunk: Once we receive the documents / we will proceed internally.
substitution_drills: Once we receive the invoice, we will process it internally.
Once we receive the report, we will review it internally.

korean: 내부 검토가 끝나면 다음 단계를 확정해서 알려드리겠습니다.
english: Once the internal review is done, we will confirm the next steps and let you know.
chunk: Once the internal review is done / we will confirm the next steps / and let you know.
pattern: Once [clause], we will [verb] and let you know.
substitution_drills: Once the budget is approved, we will finalize the scope and let you know.
Once the data is ready, we will set the timeline and let you know.

korean: 최종 제안서를 받으면 어떤 단계가 빠졌는지 내부에서 확인하고, 며칠 안에 결정해서 회신드리겠습니다.
english: Once we receive the final proposal, we will check internally which steps were removed, and we will reply with our decision within a few days.
chunk: Once we receive the final proposal / we will check internally which steps were removed / and we will reply with our decision within a few days.
pattern: Once we [verb] [object], we will [verb], and we will reply within [timeframe].
substitution_drills: Once we receive the revised quote, we will compare it with the budget, and we will reply with our choice within this week.
Once we receive the contract, we will review the terms with our team, and we will respond with comments within two days.

korean: 결론부터 말씀드리겠습니다.
english: Let me start with the conclusion.
chunk: Let me start with / the conclusion.
substitution_drills: Let me start with the summary.
Let me start with the key point.

korean: 핵심 업데이트부터 공유드리겠습니다.
english: Let me share the key update first.
chunk: Let me share the key update / first.
substitution_drills: Let me share the main result first.
Let me share the decision point first.

korean: 오늘은 데이터보다 의사결정 포인트를 중심으로 말씀드리겠습니다.
english: Today, let me focus on the decision points rather than the data.
chunk: Today / let me focus on the decision points / rather than the data.
pattern: Let me focus on [A] rather than [B].
substitution_drills: Let me focus on the risks rather than the methods.
Let me focus on the next steps rather than the background.

korean: 세부 데이터로 들어가기 전에, 결론과 필요한 결정사항부터 말씀드리겠습니다.
english: Before I go into the details, let me start with the conclusion and the decisions we need.
chunk: Before I go into the details / let me start with the conclusion / and the decisions we need.
pattern: Before I go into [detail], let me start with [A] and [B].
substitution_drills: Before I show the plots, let me start with the summary and the open questions.
Before I explain the methods, let me start with the result and the recommendation.

korean: 시간이 제한적이니, 세부 데이터는 뒤로 미루고 결론과 우리가 정해야 할 결정사항부터 말씀드리겠습니다.
english: Since our time is limited, let me leave the detailed data for later and start with the conclusion and the decisions we need to make.
chunk: Since our time is limited / let me leave the detailed data for later / and start with the conclusion and the decisions we need to make.
pattern: Since [constraint], let me leave [A] for later and start with [B].
substitution_drills: Since the agenda is full, let me leave the background for later and start with the key risks.
Since we have many topics, let me leave the methods for later and start with the recommendation.

korean: 제 권고는 기다리는 것입니다.
english: My recommendation is to wait.
chunk: My recommendation is / to wait.
substitution_drills: My recommendation is to proceed.
My recommendation is to repeat it.

korean: 제 권고는 확인 실험을 먼저 진행하는 것입니다.
english: My recommendation is to run confirmation first.
chunk: My recommendation is to run confirmation / first.
substitution_drills: My recommendation is to check specificity first.
My recommendation is to align the scope first.

korean: 제 권고는 진행하되 fallback option을 유지하는 것입니다.
english: My recommendation is to proceed while keeping a fallback option.
chunk: My recommendation is to proceed / while keeping a fallback option.
substitution_drills: My recommendation is to advance while keeping a backup target.
My recommendation is to start while keeping the second format open.

korean: 제 권고는 리드 후보를 진행하되, 백업 후보를 옵션으로 남겨두는 것입니다.
english: My recommendation is to advance the lead candidate but keep a backup as an option.
chunk: My recommendation is to advance the lead candidate / but keep a backup as an option.
pattern: My recommendation is to [verb] [A] but keep [B] as an option.
substitution_drills: My recommendation is to commit to NGS but keep Sanger as an option.
My recommendation is to fix the main scope but keep cyno testing as an option.

korean: 제 권고는 리드 후보를 진행하는 것이지만, 재현 데이터를 확인할 때까지는 백업 후보를 옵션으로 남겨두는 것이 안전하다고 봅니다.
english: My recommendation is to advance the lead candidate, but I think it is safer to keep a backup as an option until we confirm the repeat data.
chunk: My recommendation is to advance the lead candidate / but I think it is safer to keep a backup as an option / until we confirm the repeat data.
pattern: My recommendation is to [verb], but I think it is safer to [verb] until [condition].
substitution_drills: My recommendation is to start the main study, but I think it is safer to keep a pilot option until we see the first data.
My recommendation is to commit to one format, but I think it is safer to keep the second open until we confirm developability.

korean: 핵심은 차이가 유지된다는 것입니다.
english: The key point is that the difference remains.
chunk: The key point is / that the difference remains.
substitution_drills: The key point is that the trend is real.
The key point is that the signal is specific.

korean: 핵심은 후보 간 순위가 유지된다는 것입니다.
english: The key point is that the ranking between candidates remains.
chunk: The key point is / that the ranking between candidates remains.
pattern: The key point is that the [noun] remains.
substitution_drills: The key point is that the order remains.
The key point is that the separation remains.

korean: 핵심은 데이터보다 실행 가능성에 가깝습니다.
english: The key point is more about feasibility than data.
chunk: The key point is more about feasibility / than data.
pattern: The key point is more about [A] than [B].
substitution_drills: The key point is more about timing than science.
The key point is more about scope than cost.

korean: 핵심은 절대값이 아니라, 조건이 바뀌어도 순위가 유지된다는 것입니다.
english: The key point is not the absolute value, but that the ranking holds even when the conditions change.
chunk: The key point is not the absolute value / but that the ranking holds / even when the conditions change.
pattern: The key point is not [A], but that [clause] even when [condition].
substitution_drills: The key point is not the raw signal, but that the trend holds even when the format changes.
The key point is not the single value, but that the effect holds even when we repeat the run.

korean: 핵심은 어느 클론이 가장 높은가가 아니라, 두 assay에서 후보 간 순위가 일관되게 유지된다는 점이고, 그래서 이 결과를 신뢰할 수 있다고 봅니다.
english: The key point is not which clone is the highest, but that the ranking between candidates stays consistent in both assays, so I think we can trust this result.
chunk: The key point is not which clone is the highest / but that the ranking stays consistent in both assays / so I think we can trust this result.
pattern: The key point is not [A], but that [clause], so I think [conclusion].
substitution_drills: The key point is not the highest signal, but that the order stays consistent across runs, so I think the trend is real.
The key point is not the single best value, but that the separation stays consistent across formats, so I think the ranking is reliable.

korean: 안전성이 속도보다 중요합니다.
english: Safety matters more than speed.
chunk: Safety matters more / than speed.
substitution_drills: Quality matters more than speed.
Data matters more than opinion.

korean: 기능적 활성이 binding 신호보다 중요합니다.
english: Functional activity matters more than the binding signal.
chunk: Functional activity matters more / than the binding signal.
substitution_drills: Specificity matters more than the raw signal.
Reproducibility matters more than the single value.

korean: 이 단계에서는 데이터 품질이 속도보다 우선입니다.
english: At this stage, data quality matters more than speed.
chunk: At this stage / data quality matters more / than speed.
pattern: At this stage, [A] matters more than [B].
substitution_drills: At this stage, alignment matters more than progress.
At this stage, accuracy matters more than coverage.

korean: 일정이 빠듯해도, 지금은 데이터 품질이 속도보다 더 중요합니다.
english: Even if the timeline is tight, data quality matters more than speed right now.
chunk: Even if the timeline is tight / data quality matters more than speed / right now.
pattern: Even if [condition], [A] matters more than [B] right now.
substitution_drills: Even if the budget is small, safety matters more than cost right now.
Even if the pressure is high, accuracy matters more than speed right now.

korean: 일정이 빠듯한 것은 이해하지만, 이 단계에서는 데이터 품질이 속도보다 중요하기 때문에 control을 빼는 것은 권하지 않습니다.
english: I understand the timeline is tight, but at this stage data quality matters more than speed, so I would not recommend dropping the controls.
chunk: I understand the timeline is tight / but at this stage data quality matters more than speed / so I would not recommend dropping the controls.
pattern: I understand [concern], but [A] matters more than [B], so I would not recommend [action].
substitution_drills: I understand the budget is tight, but safety matters more than cost, so I would not recommend skipping the tox study.
I understand the pressure is high, but accuracy matters more than speed, so I would not recommend cutting the repeats.

korean: 정리하겠습니다.
english: To sum up.
chunk: To sum up.
substitution_drills: In short, we proceed.
In summary, we wait.

korean: 정리하면, 경향은 명확하지만 확증은 아닙니다.
english: To sum up, the trend is clear but not confirmed.
chunk: To sum up / the trend is clear but not confirmed.
substitution_drills: To sum up, the result is useful but not final.
To sum up, the data is strong but not complete.

korean: 정리하면, 진행하되 확인 실험을 병행하는 것이 제 제안입니다.
english: To sum up, my proposal is to proceed while running confirmation in parallel.
chunk: To sum up / my proposal is to proceed / while running confirmation in parallel.
pattern: To sum up, my proposal is to [verb] while [verb-ing].
substitution_drills: To sum up, my proposal is to advance while keeping a backup ready.
To sum up, my proposal is to start while confirming the trend in parallel.

korean: 정리하면, 경향은 일관되지만 한 번 더 확인이 필요하고, 그래서 백업을 유지하자는 것이 제 제안입니다.
english: To sum up, the trend is consistent but needs one more confirmation, so my proposal is to keep a backup.
chunk: To sum up / the trend is consistent but needs one more confirmation / so my proposal is to keep a backup.
pattern: To sum up, [clause], so my proposal is to [verb].
substitution_drills: To sum up, the data is promising but still early, so my proposal is to run a pilot.
To sum up, the result is clear but single-run, so my proposal is to repeat it once.

korean: 정리하면, 두 assay에서 순위가 일관되게 유지되어 결과는 신뢰할 만하지만, 직교 assay로 한 번 더 확인하기 전까지는 리드를 확정하지 말고 백업을 옵션으로 유지하자는 것이 제 제안입니다.
english: To sum up, the ranking stays consistent in both assays, so the result is reliable, but my proposal is to keep a backup as an option and not lock the lead until we confirm it with an orthogonal assay.
chunk: To sum up, the ranking stays consistent in both assays, so the result is reliable / but my proposal is to keep a backup as an option / and not lock the lead until we confirm it with an orthogonal assay.
pattern: To sum up, [clause], but my proposal is to [verb] until [condition].
substitution_drills: To sum up, the trend holds across runs, so the signal is real, but my proposal is to hold the nomination until we add a functional readout.
To sum up, the separation holds across formats, so the ranking is solid, but my proposal is to keep the second candidate open until we finish developability.
